commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer real-behavior proof plus maintainer acceptance of the backup artifact; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue from this review.; Needs attention: The latest code preserves backup file mode, but the PR still creates a new persistent transcript backup artifact that maintainers should explicitly accept. (sha=9d37372494bd396abf8a63261a0eb04e3626ecef)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds corrupt session-header recovery in `SessionManager.open`/`setSessionFile`, keeps a guard in `prepareSessionManagerForRun`, and adds focused regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +83, Tests +133. Total +216 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The linked issue and source path show a high-confidence reproduction: a malformed first JSONL header is skipped, `loadEntriesFromFile` returns `[]`, and current `setSessionFile` rewrites the file; I did not execute tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer real-behavior proof plus maintainer acceptance of the backup artifact; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue from this review.

target.title: fix(sessions): preserve corrupt-header transcripts
target.body:
## Summary

- Prevent `prepareSessionManagerForRun` from clearing an existing session transcript when the on-disk header is unreadable.
- Surface the corrupt-header condition instead, preserving the original transcript bytes for recovery/inspection.
- Add a regression test that writes a malformed first JSONL line plus a persisted user message and proves the file is unchanged after the guarded path rejects.

## Linked context

Closes #89037.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: resuming a session whose first JSONL header line is corrupted could reach the no-assistant reset path and truncate the transcript.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout at current `origin/main` with the real filesystem-backed `prepareSessionManagerForRun` helper and a temp JSONL transcript file.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true corepack pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`.
- Evidence after fix: the new regression passes and asserts the original malformed transcript string remains byte-for-byte unchanged after the helper rejects.
- Observed result after fix: the helper throws `Refusing to reset session transcript with unreadable header` before `fs.writeFile(..., "")` can run.
- What was not tested: a live production resume against a real user transcript was not performed.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: I avoided using private/local user session transcripts; the regression uses a disposable temp transcript with the same destructive file operation path.
- Before evidence: the existing no-assistant branch unconditionally called `fs.writeFile(params.sessionFile, "", "utf-8")` for any `hadSessionFile && header && !hasAssistant` state.

## Tests and validation

- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.test.ts`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true corepack pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` passed 1 Vitest shard / 3 tests.
- `git diff --check -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.test.ts` passed.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? Yes, corrupt session-header resumes now fail before destructive reset instead of silently wiping the file.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No.
- Highest-risk area: preserving the existing first-run session-manager workaround. The guard only reads and validates the existing first nonblank line before the reset; valid pre-created session headers still follow the old reset path.

## Current review state

Ready for review. No bot or reviewer comments have been addressed yet.
target.author: charles-openclaw

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: charles-openclaw
commands.review_summary:
- `recoverCorruptSessionEntries()` now stats the original transcript, writes the backup with that mode, and explicitly `chmod`s the sidecar so process umask does not leave a broader-readable copy.
- The focused regression now chmods the disposable transcript to `0o600` and asserts the generated corrupt backup is also `0o600` on non-Windows platforms.

Verification:
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/sessions/session-manager.ts src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true corepack pnpm test src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` passed 2 files / 4 tests.
- `git diff --check -- src/agents/sessions/session-manager.ts src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.test.ts`

I still cannot honestly provide a real private production resume recording from this contributor environment without using local/private session state. The disposable filesystem-backed regression now covers the recovered transcript contents plus backup permissions, and the remaining live proof item is still best done by a maintainer or a safe disposable OpenClaw session setup.

@clawsweeper re-review

commands.reason: matching ClawSweeper response comment already exists

commands.author: charles-openclaw
commands.review_summary:
- When an existing session file is non-empty but fails first-line header validation, `setSessionFile()` now backs up the original bytes to a `.corrupt-*.jsonl` sidecar before rewriting anything.
- It recovers a valid session header found later in the parsed entries, or synthesizes a fresh header when only message entries are parseable.
- It rewrites the active transcript as valid JSONL with the recovered/synthesized header plus the existing non-header entries, so the user/assistant messages survive `SessionManager.open(corruptFile)`.
- I kept the previous `prepareSessionManagerForRun` guard as the belt-and-suspenders check for that helper's reset path.

Verification:
- Added a real `SessionManager.open(sessionFile)` regression for the corrupted first-line repro shape. The test asserts the user and assistant messages remain in the active transcript and the original bytes are present in a `.corrupt-*` backup.
- Ran `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/sessions/session-manager.ts src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.test.ts`.
- Ran `PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true corepack pnpm test src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` -> 2 files / 4 tests passed.
- Ran `git diff --check -- src/agents/sessions/session-manager.ts src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.test.ts` -> passed.

@clawsweeper re-review
